exercises.title: Verification Correctness Proof
exercises.description: Prove algebraically that ML-DSA verification correctly validates signatures.
tasks: Start from the signing equations: z = y + c·s₁, t = A·s₁ + s₂
Derive what the verifier computes: w' = A·z - c·t
Show that w' relates to the original commitment w
Explain why hints are sufficient to recover w₁
Prove that forging requires solving Module-SIS
exercises.expectedOutput: Mathematical proof document showing verification correctness
hints: Substitute z = y + c·s₁ into w' = A·z - c·t
Use t = A·s₁ + s₂ to simplify
The hint corrects for the c·s₂ effect on high bits

exercises.title: UseHint Implementation
exercises.description: Implement the UseHint function and verify it works correctly with MakeHint.
tasks: Implement UseHint(h, r, alpha) that applies hint to recover correct high bits
Verify that for any r and valid hint h, UseHint correctly recovers high bits
Test edge cases near the rounding boundaries
Measure the average hint density for random inputs
Show that without the hint, verification would fail
exercises.starterCode:
Q = 8380417

def high_bits(r: int, alpha: int) -> int:
    """Extract high bits of r with rounding."""
    r = r % Q
    r0 = r % alpha
    if r0 > alpha // 2:
        r0 -= alpha
    return (r - r0) // alpha

def low_bits(r: int, alpha: int) -> int:
    """Extract low bits of r."""
    r = r % Q
    r0 = r % alpha
    if r0 > alpha // 2:
        r0 -= alpha
    return r0

def use_hint(h: int, r: int, alpha: int) -> int:
    """
    Apply hint to get correct high bits.
    
    h = 0: return high_bits(r)
    h = 1: adjust high_bits(r) based on low_bits sign
    """
    r1 = high_bits(r, alpha)
    if h == 0:
        return r1
    else:
        r0 = low_bits(r, alpha)
        # Your implementation here
        pass

# Test with ML-DSA-65 parameters
alpha = (Q - 1) // 32  # gamma2 for ML-DSA-65

# Verify hint correctness
import random
for _ in range(1000):
    # Simulate signer's view
    r_orig = random.randint(0, Q - 1)
    r1_correct = high_bits(r_orig, alpha)
    
    # Simulate verifier's view (with some offset)
    offset = random.randint(-alpha // 4, alpha // 4)
    r_verifier = (r_orig + offset) % Q
    
    # Make and use hint
    h = 1 if high_bits(r_verifier, alpha) != r1_correct else 0
    r1_recovered = use_hint(h, r_verifier, alpha)
    
    assert r1_recovered == r1_correct, "Hint failed!"
exercises.expectedOutput: Working UseHint with verification against MakeHint
hints: When h=1 and r0 > 0, increment r1
When h=1 and r0 <= 0, decrement r1
Handle wrap-around at the modulus boundary

exercises.title: Verification Timing Analysis
exercises.description: Analyze and compare verification timing across different inputs.
tasks: Measure verification time for 100 valid signatures
Measure verification time for 100 invalid signatures (modified)
Check if timing differs between valid and invalid cases
Analyze timing variance and identify potential side channels
Compare with signing time measurements
exercises.expectedOutput: Timing analysis showing constant-time verification behavior
hints: Verification should be constant-time regardless of validity
Early rejection for decoding errors may have different timing
Focus on the core verification computation timing

exercises.title: Verification Step Trace
exercises.description: Trace through a complete verification with toy parameters.
tasks: Using toy parameters (n=4, k=2, l=2, q=17), create a valid signature
Trace each verification step with concrete numerical values
Show the hint application step explicitly
Verify the challenge comparison succeeds
Demonstrate failure when signature is modified
hints: Use the signed example from the signing lesson
Highlight where hints correct the high bits
